title: Sujebi Korean Noodle Soup
description: A comforting, molten Korean noodle soup that's perfect for a solo dinner. This recipe is inspired by the traditional gyeonggi province's take on sujebi.

ingredients.name: Vegetable broth
ingredients.notes: You can use a store-bought broth or make your own from vegetable scraps.

ingredients.name: Cilantro
ingredients.notes: Chopped cilantro adds freshness to this hearty soup.

ingredients.name: Gochujang
ingredients.notes: This Korean chili paste adds depth and heat to the soup.

ingredients.name: Sesame oil
ingredients.notes: A drizzle of sesame oil adds nutty flavor to the soup.

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: Use a high-quality salt for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Black pepper
ingredients.notes: Freshly ground black pepper enhances the flavor of the soup.

instructions: Combine the vegetable broth and gochujang in a large pot and bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce the heat to low and add the Korean instant noodles. Simmer for 10 minutes, or until the noodles are tender.
Stir in the chopped cilantro, sesame oil, salt, and black pepper.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve hot, garnished with toasted sesame seeds if desired.
